verses.text: When a man or woman separates by a Nazirite vow — abstain from wine, strong drink, vinegar, grape juice, anything of the vine. No razor on the head; the locks grow. Not come near a dead body. Holy unto the LORD. Guidance: A Nazirite — male or female — chooses to live like a priest for a season. Three signs: no wine, no haircut, no death. A woman could vow this; Samson's mother carried it for him from the womb.

If someone dies suddenly beside him and defiles the consecration — shave the head the seventh day. The eighth day bring two turtledoves to the priest; one for sin, one for burnt; atone, hallow the head. Consecrate the days again; bring a lamb for trespass. Former days lost. Guidance: Even an accidental closeness to death restarts the clock. The vow is fragile — but God provides the reset. Failure doesn't end the consecration; it just begins it again.

When the days are fulfilled — bring offerings: lamb for burnt, ewe for sin, ram for peace, unleavened cakes, drink offering. Priest offers at the tabernacle door. Shave the head; the hair goes on the fire under the peace offering. Priest takes the shoulder, cake, wafer; waves before the LORD. After, the Nazirite may drink wine. Guidance: The vow ends in a feast. The grown hair burns in the fire — the visible sign released back to God. The vow opens with abstinence and closes with celebration.

The LORD: speak to Aaron and sons — bless Israel: The LORD bless thee and keep thee; make His face shine upon thee and be gracious; lift up His countenance and give thee peace. They shall put My name on Israel, and I will bless them. Guidance: Six blessings in three lines. Bless and keep, shine and be gracious, lift His face and give peace. The priests do the speaking; God does the blessing. The benediction has carried God's people for three thousand years.
